2604.14967.title: UniDoc-RL: Coarse-to-Fine Visual RAG with Hierarchical Actions and Dense Rewards
2604.14967.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) extends Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) with external visual knowledge. However, existing visual RAG systems typically rely on generic retrieval signals that overlook the fine-grained visual semantics essential for complex reasoning. To address this limitation, we propose UniDoc-RL, a unified reinforcement learning framework in which an LVLM agent jointly performs retrieval, reranking, active visual perception, and reasoning. UniDoc-RL formulates visual information acquisition as a sequential decision-making problem with a hierarchical action space. Specifically, it progressively refines visual evidence from coarse-grained document retrieval to fine-grained image selection and active region cropping, allowing the model to suppress irrelevant content and attend to information-dense regions. For effective end-to-end training, we introduce a dense multi-reward scheme that provides task-aware supervision for each action. Based on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), UniDoc-RL aligns agent behavior with multiple objectives without relying on a separate value network. To support this training paradigm, we curate a comprehensive dataset of high-quality reasoning trajectories with fine-grained action annotations. Experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate that UniDoc-RL consistently surpasses state-of-the-art baselines, yielding up to 17.7% gains over prior RL-based methods.
2604.14967.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）擴展了大型視覺-語言模型（LVLMs）與外部視覺知識。<lb/>然而，現有的視覺 RAG 系統通常依賴於通用的檢索信號，忽略了對於複雜推理至關重要的細緻視覺語義。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提出了 UniDoc-RL，一個統一的強化學習框架，其中 LVLM 代理共同執行檢索、重新排序、主動視覺感知和推理。<lb/>UniDoc-RL 將視覺信息獲取形式化為一個具有層次行動空間的序列決策問題。<lb/>具體而言，它逐步從粗粒度的文檔檢索精煉到細粒度的圖像選擇和主動區域裁剪，允許模型抑制無關內容並關注信息密集的區域。<lb/>為了有效的端到端訓練，我們引入了一個密集的多重獎勵方案，為每個行動提供任務感知的監督。<lb/>基於群體相對策略優化（GRPO），UniDoc-RL 將代理行為與多個目標對齊，而不依賴於單獨的價值網絡。<lb/>為了支持這一訓練範式，我們整理了一個高質量推理軌跡的綜合數據集，並附有細粒度的行動註釋。<lb/>在三個基準上的實驗表明，UniDoc-RL 始終超越最先進的基準，較之前的基於強化學習的方法提高了最多 17.7% 的性能。
2604.14967.author: Jun Wang et.al.
2604.14967.authors: Jun Wang, Shuo Tan, Zelong Sun, Tiancheng Gu, Yongle Zhao, Ziyong Feng, Kaicheng Yang, Zhiwu Lu
